actionable_feedback: Make the spreadsheet fully inspectable: ensure it is saved in a standard .xlsx format (no macros/embedded objects) so openpyxl can parse it in automated checks. Re-run the precheck to confirm parse_status is not 'openpyxl_unavailable'.

Add a 'source_map' or 'index' sheet in ripple_root_cause_matrix.xlsx that maps each derived-column cell range to the exact input/task.json packet field (e.g., Sheet1!G2 -> input/task.json:/records:R1.spool_error_pct).

Expose cell-level score and remediation formulas in the spreadsheet (no hard-coded precomputed values only). Where values are precomputed for readability, include adjacent formula cells or comments with the exact formulas and cell references.

Expand corrective_action.md to include explicit normalization/unit-conversion steps and link those steps to the specific cells in the workbook (e.g., 'normalization factor in Sheet:Normalizations!B4, applied to column C').
Clarify threshold tie-break policy (e.g., 'score == 70.0 treated as Warn or Fail') and include one worked example showing classification and +/-5% perturbation at the boundary.

Document remediation interaction semantics (additive vs multiplicative vs conservative combination) and show one end-to-end recomputation for the recommended combo (O1+O2) on the release-controlling record.
Fix minor rounding display inconsistencies (e.g., ensure chosen display precision is documented and consistent between workbook and report).
Update final_message.txt to indicate that spreadsheet parseability has been verified and list the updated locations of explicit cell-to-packet mappings and examples to speed re-verification.

Add an explicit source_map sheet and ensure cells contain live formulas with traceable references to the input packet fields.'}

{'artifact': 'attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]', 'comment': 'Report documents the scoring formula and per-record calculations. Expand with explicit cell references for normalization and remediation, clarify threshold equality handling, and include a recomputed example showing remediation combination impact.'}
failed_or_weak_rubric_items: {'rubric_id': 'ri_2', 'issue': 'Score-matrix header mapping to packet fields is absent (discoverability gap).', 'severity': 'medium'}
failed_rubric_items: {'rubric_id': 'ri_2', 'issue': 'Score-matrix header mapping to packet fields is absent (discoverability gap).', 'severity': 'medium'}
feedback_summary: Baseline delivered required artifacts and passed artifact-contract checks (final_score 0.845). The report and spreadsheet contain the scoring formula, per-record calculations, sensitivity analysis, remediation combos, and budget checks. Remaining issues are programmatic inspectability of the .xlsx (openpyxl_unavailable prevented formula-level verification) and a few discoverability/documentation gaps: explicit normalization/unit-conversion justification, a score-matrix header that maps spreadsheet columns to packet fields, tie-break policy at thresholds, and explicit remediation-interaction semantics for overlapping options. Minor numeric rounding presentation differences were observed but do not affect classifications. Manual opening of the workbook to confirm cell-level formulas and explicit cell-to-packet mappings is recommended for final verification.

notable_verifier_issues.message: Spreadsheet formulas could not be programmatically inspected because the artifact preview reports openpyxl_unavailable. Manual open recommended.
Normalization/unit-basis concerns flagged as needs_review in corrective_action.md.
Trivial rounding difference for R2 (64.464 vs 64.4645) observed; no classification impact.

extra_model_fields.release_decision: Hold (R1 remains failure after best budget-valid remediation)

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce a revised attempt (351-revised) that: 1) updates ripple_root_cause_matrix.xlsx with an index/source_map sheet and explicit cell formulas (not only prose), 2) ensures the workbook is saved so automated tools (openpyxl) can parse it, 3) expands corrective_action.md with normalization/unit-conversion justification, explicit cell references, tie-break policy, remediation interaction model and a recomputed worked example, 4) corrects minor rounding presentation inconsistencies and 5) updates final_message.txt to confirm parseability and point reviewers to the new mapping and examples.

Steps demonstrate reading skills and generating artifacts but do not replace the need to make the workbook programmatically parsable for automated verification.
